title: rubus × acutatus
intro: Rubus × acutatus, also known as the hybrid thimbleberry, is a cross between the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus), resulting in a unique and edible fruit-bearing plant. The medicinal properties of Rubus × acutatus include its high content of antioxidants, which help protect against cell damage and inflammation. The health benefits of consuming Rubus × acutatus include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, improve cognitive function, and support digestive health. Rubus × acutatus can be prepared as an herbal tea, jam, or supplement, and its fruit can be eaten fresh or used in baked goods, making it a versatile and nutritious addition to a healthy diet.
